seoTitle: How to Edit a Typeform After Creating It
metaDescription: Learn how to update questions, answer choices, themes, and logic in an existing Typeform, then preview your changes before publishing.
intro: Forms rarely stay the same once you publish them, whether you need to fix a question or refresh the design. This guide walks through how to edit an existing Typeform, from updating questions and answer choices to adjusting themes and logic, without disrupting the form's overall structure.

steps.heading: Open your Typeform
steps.body: Go into your Typeform workspace and select the form you want to update so you can access its editor.

steps.heading: Edit questions and answers
steps.body: Modify existing questions, change answer choices, or add new ones directly in the editor while keeping the rest of the form intact.

steps.heading: Update design and logic
steps.body: Adjust themes, branding, and layout as needed, and revisit any logic or conditional branching affected by your question changes.

steps.heading: Preview and publish changes
steps.body: Use the preview mode to check how your edits look and flow before publishing them live to respondents.

faq.question: Will editing questions affect responses I've already collected?
faq.answer: Changing or removing a question after responses are collected can make historical data harder to interpret alongside new responses, so it's worth reviewing existing logic and reports carefully.

faq.question: Can I preview edits before making them live?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform lets you preview your form so you can check questions, design, and logic before publishing changes.

faq.question: Do edits affect my form's overall structure?
faq.answer: Typeform is designed to let you edit questions, themes, and logic without breaking the form's overall structure, though major changes to logic should still be tested.

faq.question: Can I edit a Typeform that's already been shared?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit a live form at any time, and changes take effect once you publish them.
